text:
human forms. They are irritable and resentful, and they punish with
ulcers, tetter, ringworms, pimples, and other cutaneous affections, all
those who neglect them at brewings, bakings, and feastings. They punish
in a similar manner those who enter new houses without making obeisance
to the four corners, and paying them other kindly attentions; those
who live in untidy houses are also likewise punished. The Kirkonwæki
(church-folk) are little deformed beings living under the altars of
churches. These misshapen things are supposed to be able to aid their
sorrowing and suffering worshipers.
